source.pdf: official_books/Nimble 5e TTRPG Heroes Core Book -- Evan Diaz -- 41845a084350bb7daa48eb313815b1f9 -- Anna’s Archive.pdf

class.name: The Cheat
class.description:
Cloak and dagger... and dagger. The common Cheat does not call herself a Cheat, of course-rather, a streetsmart rogue, a survivalist, or a liberator of unused goods. They are masters of stealth, pickpocketing, and smooth talking. Some of the more vicious ilk are masters of death-dealing as well.
Cheats can be found in every city and byway under countless different names, but they all believe something similar: It is a cruel, merciless world, and those who aim to survive do not have time for silly things like morals or honor. Those are luxuries of the rich and powerful. Honor gets you killed-survival is about taking what you want, when you can.

progression.name: Sneak Attack
progression.combatUsageNotes: Treat this as a trigger-based tool: hold it until the stated condition happens, then fire it for swing value. Budget around its 1/TURN limit so it lands in the highest-impact window.

progression.name: Vicious Opportunist
progression.description: (1/turn) When you hit a Distracted target with a melee attack, you may change the Primary Die roll to whatever you like (changing it to the max value counts as a crit). A target is Distracted if it is adjacent to or Taunted by an ally, or if it cannot see you.
progression.combatUsageNotes: Treat this as a trigger-based tool: hold it until the stated condition happens, then fire it for swing value. Budget around its 1/TURN limit so it lands in the highest-impact window.

progression.name: Cheat
progression.description: You are a well-rounded cheater: (1/round) move or hide for free; (1/day) change any skill check to 10+INT; if you roll less than 10 on initiative, change it to 10; gain advantage on game, competition, and wager skill checks.
progression.combatUsageNotes: Use this for positioning: engage priority targets, escape danger, or set up advantage before attacking. Budget around its 1/ROUND limit so it lands in the highest-impact window.

progression.name: Subclass
progression.combatUsageNotes: Pick options that support your current build plan; favor synergy over isolated power.

progression.name: Sneak Attack (2)
progression.combatUsageNotes: Track this resource every turn and spend it proactively instead of ending encounters with unused value.

progression.name: Thieves' Cant
progression.description: You learn the secret language of rogues and scoundrels.
progression.combatUsageNotes: Use this in the rounds where its trigger/conditions are easiest to satisfy for reliable combat value.

progression.name: Low Blow
progression.description: When you Sneak Attack, you may spend 2 additional actions to Incapacitate your target for their next turn on a failed STR save (DC 10+INT). Save or fail, they are Taunted by you until you drop to 0 HP.
progression.combatUsageNotes: Treat this as a trigger-based tool: hold it until the stated condition happens, then fire it for swing value.

progression.name: Sweet Talk
progression.description: You may gain advantage on all Influence checks with NPCs you have just met for the first time. This lasts until you fail an Influence check with them or until you meet a second time. You have disadvantage on Influence checks with them after you use this ability (until you get back on their good side).
progression.combatUsageNotes: Treat this as a trigger-based tool: hold it until the stated condition happens, then fire it for swing value.

progression.name: Amidst All This Commotion...
progression.description: If a creature dies while you Sneak Attack them, you may turn Invisible until you attack again or until the beginning of your next turn.
progression.combatUsageNotes: Use this in the rounds where its trigger/conditions are easiest to satisfy for reliable combat value.

progression.name: Leave No Trace
progression.description: Advantage on Stealth checks when you are at full health.
progression.combatUsageNotes: Treat this as a trigger-based tool: hold it until the stated condition happens, then fire it for swing value.

progression.name: Key Stat Increase
progression.combatUsageNotes: Take the stat that supports your core combat loop and your most frequent checks/attack scaling.

progression.name: Underhanded Ability
progression.combatUsageNotes: Pick options that support your current build plan; favor synergy over isolated power.

progression.name: Trade Secrets
progression.description: Whenever you spend a night talking shop with other roguish types during a Safe Rest, you may choose different Cheat options available to you.
progression.combatUsageNotes: Use this in the rounds where its trigger/conditions are easiest to satisfy for reliable combat value.

progression.name: Twist the Blade
progression.description: Action: Change one of your Sneak Attack dice to whatever you like.
progression.combatUsageNotes: Plan your action economy around this; use it on turns where its payoff beats a standard attack.

progression.name: Quick Read
progression.description: (1/encounter) Gain advantage on an Assess check. (1/day) Gain advantage on an Examination check.
progression.combatUsageNotes: Use this in the rounds where its trigger/conditions are easiest to satisfy for reliable combat value. Budget around its 1/ENCOUNTER limit so it lands in the highest-impact window.

progression.name: Secondary Stat Increase
progression.combatUsageNotes: Take the stat that supports your core combat loop and your most frequent checks/attack scaling.

progression.name: Underhanded Ability (2)
progression.combatUsageNotes: Pick options that support your current build plan; favor synergy over isolated power.

progression.name: THAT'S Not What Happened!
progression.description: (1/Safe Rest) Action: After a Distracted enemy attacks you, you may change the Primary Die roll to whatever you like (changing the die to the minimum value counts as a miss).
progression.combatUsageNotes: Treat this as a trigger-based tool: hold it until the stated condition happens, then fire it for swing value. Budget around its 1/SAFE REST limit so it lands in the highest-impact window.

progression.name: Subclass
progression.combatUsageNotes: Pick options that support your current build plan; favor synergy over isolated power.

progression.name: Sneak Attack (3)
progression.combatUsageNotes: Track this resource every turn and spend it proactively instead of ending encounters with unused value.

progression.name: Pocket Sand
progression.description: (2/encounter) When you Defend against a melee attack, Blind the attacker until the start of their next turn and force them to reroll the attack (Blinded creatures attack with disadvantage).
progression.combatUsageNotes: Treat this as a trigger-based tool: hold it until the stated condition happens, then fire it for swing value. Budget around its 2/ENCOUNTER limit so it lands in the highest-impact window.

progression.name: Cunning Strike
progression.description: (2/encounter) When you land a Sneak Attack, you may force the target to make a STR save (DC 10+INT). On a failure, instead of rolling your Sneak Attack dice, they deal the maximum amount of damage (if your target saves, regain 1 use).
progression.combatUsageNotes: Treat this as a trigger-based tool: hold it until the stated condition happens, then fire it for swing value. Budget around its 2/ENCOUNTER limit so it lands in the highest-impact window.

progression.name: Underhanded Ability (3)
progression.combatUsageNotes: Pick options that support your current build plan; favor synergy over isolated power.

progression.combatUsageNotes: Take the stat that supports your core combat loop and your most frequent checks/attack scaling.

progression.name: Sneak Attack (5)

progression.name: Escape Plan
progression.description: (1/Safe Rest) When you would drop to 0 HP or gain a Wound, you do not. Instead, you turn Invisible for 1 minute or until you attack.
progression.combatUsageNotes: Treat this as a trigger-based tool: hold it until the stated condition happens, then fire it for swing value. Budget around its 1/SAFE REST limit so it lands in the highest-impact window.

progression.name: Professional Skulker
progression.description: Gain a climbing speed and advantage on Stealth checks (replaces Leave No Trace).
progression.combatUsageNotes: Use this for positioning: engage priority targets, escape danger, or set up advantage before attacking.

progression.name: Twist the Blade (2)
progression.description: (1/turn) You can Twist the Blade for free.
progression.combatUsageNotes: Use this in the rounds where its trigger/conditions are easiest to satisfy for reliable combat value. Budget around its 1/TURN limit so it lands in the highest-impact window.

progression.name: Sneak Attack (6)
progression.combatUsageNotes: Track this resource every turn and spend it proactively instead of ending encounters with unused value.

progression.name: Heads I Win, Tails You Lose
progression.description: (1/encounter) Attacks you make this round do not miss, you crit on 1 less than normally needed, and you gain LVL temp HP.
progression.combatUsageNotes: Pair this with crit-fishing turns and high-accuracy setups to maximize burst value. Budget around its 1/ENCOUNTER limit so it lands in the highest-impact window.

progression.name: KILL
progression.description: When you crit an enemy with fewer max HP than you, it dies.
progression.combatUsageNotes: Treat this as a trigger-based tool: hold it until the stated condition happens, then fire it for swing value.

progression.name: Epic Boon
progression.description: Choose an Epic Boon (see pg. 23 of the GM's Guide).
progression.combatUsageNotes: Pick options that support your current build plan; favor synergy over isolated power.

progression.name: Supreme Execution
progression.description: +1 to any 2 of your stats. When you attack with a blade, you do not require targets to be Distracted to trigger Vicious Opportunist.
progression.combatUsageNotes: Treat this as a trigger-based tool: hold it until the stated condition happens, then fire it for swing value.

choiceGroups.name: Underhanded Abilities
choiceGroups.description: Primary selectable ability pool for The Cheat.

choiceOptions.name: Creative Accounting
choiceOptions.description: Steal up to INT actions from your next turn (gain up to INT actions now; the next time you would gain actions, subtract the number stolen). You cannot use this 2 turns in a row.
choiceOptions.combatUsageNotes: Use this in the rounds where its trigger/conditions are easiest to satisfy for reliable combat value.

choiceOptions.name: Exploit Weakness
choiceOptions.description: Action: Make a contested INT check against an enemy. If you win, you can use Vicious Opportunist against them even if they are not Distracted. This lasts for 1 minute or until you use this ability against another target.
choiceOptions.combatUsageNotes: Plan your action economy around this; use it on turns where its payoff beats a standard attack.

choiceOptions.name: Feinting Attack
choiceOptions.description: If you miss for the 2nd time in a single round, you may change the primary die roll to any result instead.
choiceOptions.combatUsageNotes: Use this in the rounds where its trigger/conditions are easiest to satisfy for reliable combat value.

choiceOptions.name: How'd YOU get here?!
choiceOptions.description: 2 actions: Teleport up to 4 spaces away, adjacent to a Distracted target, and make a melee attack against them. If you crit, you may teleport again.
choiceOptions.combatUsageNotes: Plan your action economy around this; use it on turns where its payoff beats a standard attack.

choiceOptions.name: I'm Outta Here!
choiceOptions.description: When an ally within 4 spaces is crit, you may turn invisible until the end of your next turn and then move up to half your speed for free.
choiceOptions.combatUsageNotes: Treat this as a trigger-based tool: hold it until the stated condition happens, then fire it for swing value.

choiceOptions.name: Misdirection
choiceOptions.description: Gain INT armor. Whenever you Defend, you may halve the damage instead.
choiceOptions.combatUsageNotes: Save this for enemy spike turns; it is strongest when it prevents a big tempo or survivability loss.

choiceOptions.name: Steal Tempo
choiceOptions.description: When you land a critical hit for the second time on a turn, your target loses 1 action and you gain 1 action.
choiceOptions.combatUsageNotes: Treat this as a trigger-based tool: hold it until the stated condition happens, then fire it for swing value.

choiceOptions.name: Sunder Armor (Medium)
choiceOptions.description: Action: When you crit an enemy with medium armor, sunder their armor. Until the start of your next turn, all melee attacks against that target ignore its armor.
choiceOptions.combatUsageNotes: Treat this as a trigger-based tool: hold it until the stated condition happens, then fire it for swing value.

choiceOptions.name: Sunder Armor (Heavy)
choiceOptions.description: Req. Sunder Armor (Medium). Your Sunder Armor ability now also applies to enemies wearing heavy armor.
choiceOptions.combatUsageNotes: Save this for enemy spike turns; it is strongest when it prevents a big tempo or survivability loss.

choiceOptions.name: Trickshot
choiceOptions.description: When you throw a dagger, it returns to your hand at the end of your turn. On a hit, it ricochets to another creature within 2 spaces, dealing half as much damage to them.
choiceOptions.combatUsageNotes: Treat this as a trigger-based tool: hold it until the stated condition happens, then fire it for swing value.

choiceOptions.name: Amidst All This Commotion...
choiceOptions.description: If a creature dies while you Sneak Attack them, you may turn Invisible until you attack again or until the beginning of your next turn.
choiceOptions.combatUsageNotes: Unlocks at level 3. Use this in the rounds where its trigger/conditions are easiest to satisfy for reliable combat value.

choiceOptions.name: Leave No Trace
choiceOptions.description: Advantage on Stealth checks when you are at full health.
choiceOptions.combatUsageNotes: Unlocks at level 3. Treat this as a trigger-based tool: hold it until the stated condition happens, then fire it for swing value.

choiceOptions.combatUsageNotes: Unlocks at level 7. Treat this as a trigger-based tool: hold it until the stated condition happens, then fire it for swing value. Budget around its 2/ENCOUNTER limit so it lands in the highest-impact window.

choiceOptions.description: Gain a climbing speed and advantage on Stealth checks (replaces Leave No Trace).

You have disadvantage on Influence checks with them after you use this ability (until you get back on their good side).
